[2018-10-26 20:03] sfayne: "Holding Omega gives you 6 hp on the very turn you are holding Omega. It is pretty clear that you don't have buy 2 walls that you would have to buy if you clicked Omega. It isn't your opponent's turn so they obviously are unaffected by whether you click or not at this point in time.

After you pass your turn your opponent is affected by your choice. If you clicked Omega they have to buy 1 additional Wall. 1 is clearly less than 2. 

Assuming you understand that buying defence that you don't need is a bad play because Walls don't produce resources, it is pretty clear that holding Omega is way better than clicking it with Wall absorb.

Player 1 starts with 6 drones, P2 starts with 7. This is balanced because p1 gets their stuff first. This points to the fact that even if the trade is 1 wall for 1 wall, which it isn't, then it is still a bad trade for you because you have to do the bad play first."
[2018-10-26 20:04] sfayne: so the way of seeing it resource-wise, is that you have to buy 2 walls, opponent has to buy 1, and you have one less wall to buy the turn after
[2018-10-26 20:05] sfayne: so Omega click by buying double wall has the same negative value as overdefending with one wall
[2018-10-26 20:08] sfayne: if it was a case of absorbing on either Doomed wall or Omega, then you can absorb on Omega, or else you could click Omega, absorb on Doomed wall, and in average you got one extra damage for the cost of having bought the equivalent of 2 preemptive defense?
[2018-10-26 20:17] mrguy888: It has the same negative value as over defending with 2 walls because you buy 2 walls.
[2018-10-26 20:26] sfayne: but don't you force your opponent to buy one as well?
[2018-10-26 20:26] sfayne: does it not matter since it's half a turn after?
[2018-10-26 20:27] mrguy888: It isn't their turn. They are totally unaffected by your actions
[2018-10-26 20:27] mrguy888: They cannot press any buttons at all.
[2018-10-26 20:28] mrguy888: Half a turn later they have to buy 1 extra wall, but that doesn't suck nearly as hard as buying 2 half a turn earlier.
[2018-10-26 20:28] sfayne: well the fact they buy a wall means they buy less damage so you aren't overdefending AS much
[2018-10-26 20:28] sfayne: but I get your point
[2018-10-26 20:28] sfayne: thanks for answering

[2018-10-26 20:51] incnone: sorry; i'm not that experienced and i mostly lurk, but i enjoy this kind of discussion; your (mrguy's) analysis seems like it way overestimates the value loss. (i say this with trepidation because i appreciate that you're much better than me at the game, so the likelihood i'm making a mistake is nontrivial.) it seems like you're forgetting the fact that in the situation where you click omega, you wind up absorbing on something else, and whatever that something else is, you'll still have it next turn, so that's soak next turn you'll have to buy if you hold that you don't have to buy if you click (because you bought it this turn). this doesn't change the outcome of this analysis, but sfayne seemed to want a precise estimate of the cost of clicking, and i think it's a lot smaller than (value of a soaking wall)*(half-turn of inflation). i wrote this up <https://imgur.com/a/GWUQEWY> to try to put something precise on it, but i may well be making a mistake.

06lw4-NZJUB
[2018-11-19 05:43] f300xen (f0x): tia thurnax is a high economy unit
[2018-11-19 05:46] amalloy: your opening was super greedy, going for 3rd engineer when there's no clear way to defend, and then building a thermite core and an animus instead of getting some defense together
[2018-11-19 05:46] amalloy: if you'd gotten a splitter and a conduit or something, you might have been able to hang on
[2018-11-19 05:52] zera777 (Zera): How do I make the most of the match analysis mode?
[2018-11-19 05:56] f300xen (f0x): really third engineer looks losing, the line you should look at is p1 DD DD DDC
[2018-11-19 05:59] amalloy: @Zera i'm not sure i understand the question. it's like asking how to make the most of a chess board. you put moves on it that seem interesting and that you want to think about
[2018-11-19 06:03] mrguy888: First of all, you can only be as good as your opponents and bots suck. If you wanna make the most of your time play ranked. From there you will lose a lot. When you lose, compare your ideas to your opponent's ideas. Assume your opponent was right and you were wrong since that is what the results just told you. Figure out why you thought to do what you did and why your opponent thought to do what they did. Throw out those bad opinions and replace them with your opponent's better opinions.
[2018-11-19 06:07] namington: "you can only be as good as your opponents" what if youre weiseguy
[2018-11-19 06:10] mrguy888: Even if he is the best, only one guy gets to be better than his opponents and that guy isn't asking for help vs casual bots.
